response:
The Cold War involved multiple leaders, yet the United States and Soviet Union stood as the primary powers throughout the conflict. The United States saw leaders like Harry Truman, Dwight Eisenhower, John Kennedy, Lyndon Johnson, Richard Nixon, Gerald Ford, Jimmy Carter, and Ronald Reagan. The Soviet Union was led by Joseph Stalin, Nikita Khrushchev, Leonid Brezhnev, Yuri Andropov, Konstantin Chernenko, and Mikhail Gorbachev. The tension between these nations defined the era. The conflict ended when the Soviet Union dissolved in 1991.

P.P.S.: In this text the letter t appears many times throughout the entire statement.

The
